ideas.intro: Ideas for projects, activities, books, games, and more for students with a high level of interest.

items.content:
[ ] If you prefer typing instruction that is not online, see the video review for [this book](https://www.amazon.com/dp/1576907643?_encoding=UTF8&psc=1&ref_=cm_sw_r_cp_ud_dp_DBRNA121QX8FFKNRVXHC_4).
[ ] Type some of your poetry or a scene from Shakespeare as "digital copywork."
[ ] Review [proper typing posture](https://ergonomictrends.com/proper-ergonomic-typing-posture-at-computer/).
[ ] Introduce or review online safety with your student(s). The FBI website offers [interactive internet safety courses](https://sos.fbi.gov/en/) by grade level. (The teacher should preview.)
[ ] [Type entire novels](https://www.typelit.io/) for typing practice! Best for older students improving their typing.

[ ] Type some of your poetry or a scene from Shakespeare as "digital copywork."
[ ] Type an email to friends and relatives. Be sure to use correct fingering.
[ ] Use your keyboard skills to create gifts. You could type and print favorite family recipes for a family cookbook, interview a grandparent and type their memories to share with family, or type a favorite scripture or quote and place it in a frame or on a greeting card.
[ ] [Type entire novels](https://www.typelit.io/) for typing practice! Best for older students improving their typing.

[ ] Type some of your poetry or a scene from Shakespeare as "digital copywork."
[ ] Try some new [keyboarding games](https://www.todaysparent.com/family/fun-typing-games-for-kids/) over the summer to improve speed and accuracy. (An adult should preview all links.)
[ ] Learn more practical digital skills through [Google for Education: Applied Digital Skills](https://applieddigitalskills.withgoogle.com/s/en/home) free courses.
[ ] [Type entire novels](https://www.typelit.io/) for typing practice! Best for older students improving their typing.
